profile_evidence.read: The selected-limit HSK bf16 producer is dominated by the active-group accumulator fold in LDS. Global VMEM writes are already heavily reduced in this branch, so the next useful kernel work should reduce LDS update/conflict pressure, not just lower final global atomic count.

accum_stride_pad_1.s4096_reason: S4096 max_active_groups=512 already uses the MI350 dynamic shared-memory limit. Padding the accumulator row by one float requests 165888 bytes versus the 163840-byte device limit.

accum_slot_swizzle_1.read: The simple slot/D swizzle is correct but not retained. It is slower on the small correctness point, and the S4096 same-benchmark comparison perturbs the HSK bf16 baseline relative to the clean baseline-only 5.105 ms run.

decision.next_kernel_direction: Keep the fast HSK bf16 source-fed producer, but replace the single [max_active_groups, block_d] shared accumulator fold with a different ownership/reduction strategy: fewer active groups per CTA, wave-private or lane-group accumulators before LDS, or a two-stage local reduction that lowers repeated LDS read-modify-write pressure. Re-profile SQ_LDS_BANK_CONFLICT and SQ_INSTS_LDS after that change.
